# Simon Scrapes / Agentic Academy — Gold-Standard Voice Samples
*Generated by brand-voice skill — 2026-03-10*
*Enriched from previous brand guidelines, landing page, and YouTube patterns*

These sentences represent the voice at its most authentic.
Organized by platform/medium for easy reference when writing for a specific channel.

---

## YouTube — Tutorial narration
"So, to demonstrate, here's all the data I just pulled..."
*Why it's representative: "So," connector opener, show-don't-tell, insider sharing energy. This is peak YouTube Simon.*

## YouTube — Genuine excitement
"And the crazy thing is it's super cheap, too."
*Why it's representative: Connector ("And") + intensifier ("super cheap") + genuine surprise. Natural, unscripted energy.*

## YouTube — Intensifier pattern
"This is so, so easy."
*Why it's representative: Double-word intensifier — the signature emphasis pattern. Sounds like someone genuinely impressed, not performing.*

## YouTube — Calling out complexity
"Now, this is the closest you're going to get to actually coding... Again, this might seem a little bit complex, but you can use Perplexity and Claude..."
*Why it's representative: Acknowledges difficulty honestly, then immediately offers the solution. Builds trust by not pretending everything is easy.*

## YouTube — Teacher directive voice
"All you're going to do is... So, you're going to go straight to... You just need to make sure that..."
*Why it's representative: Step-by-step guidance using direct "you" address. Not lecturing — walking alongside.*

## YouTube — Personal struggle
"I know personally I've wasted a ton of hours..."
*Why it's representative: Raw relatability. Not a faceless expert — a real person who's been through it. Builds immediate trust.*

---

## Landing page — Hero headline
"Skip the 2-year learning curve — build agentic systems that actually run your business, today."
*Why it's representative: Bold timeframe claim, benefit-first, casual "today" landing. Sets the tone for everything.*

## Landing page — Body copy
"Everything runs using your brand voice. No AI slop. Same quality you'd produce yourself, without the 4hrs of work."
*Why it's representative: Anti-slop positioning front and centre. Specific time saved. The two sentences that most define the brand.*

## Landing page — Benefit list
"The workflow you've been 'meaning to build' → live and running"
*Why it's representative: Uses quotes to acknowledge the reader's inner monologue. Arrow for transformation. Playful.*

## Landing page — Proof section
"Copy-paste agentic systems — plug them in and they work today"
*Why it's representative: "Copy-paste" removes intimidation. "Today" creates urgency. Zero jargon barrier.*

---

## LinkedIn — Problem opener
"We've all been there — spending 3 hours every week on a task that should take 30 minutes."
*Why it's representative: "We've all been there" — collaborative, relatable. Specific numbers (3 hours, 30 minutes). Empathy before solution.*

## LinkedIn — Simple solution format
"Here's what actually works: ✅ Connect your 3 main data sources ✅ Set up one simple dashboard ✅ Automate the weekly refresh ✅ Done"
*Why it's representative: "Here's what actually works" — insider authority. Checkmarks. "Done" as a mic-drop fragment.*

## LinkedIn — Results with warmth
"But here's the best part — their Friday afternoons are free again."
*Why it's representative: "But" connector. "Here's the best part" transition. The benefit is human (free time), not technical.*

## LinkedIn — Collaborative closer
"What manual process is draining time from your team's week?"
*Why it's representative: Ends with a question, not a command. Invites dialogue. Specific enough to make people think.*

---

## Email — Subject line
"The 2-hour fix that saves 10 hours weekly"
*Why it's representative: Specific numbers in subject. Time investment vs time saved. No hype words.*

## Email — Personal opener
"Quick question: How much time did you spend last week on tasks that could have been automated?"
*Why it's representative: Conversational, direct, makes the reader think about their own situation immediately.*

## Email — P.S. teaser
"P.S. — Next week I'll share the exact 3-tool setup we used (spoiler: you probably already have 2 of them)."
*Why it's representative: Insider knowledge tease. Parenthetical aside for personality. "You probably already have" removes intimidation.*

---

## Twitter/X — Hot take
"Stop building complex workflows. Start with the one manual task that wastes the most time. Fix that first. Then move to the next one."
*Why it's representative: Direct, punchy, opinionated. Short fragments. Practical, not theoretical. "Fix that first" is peak directive voice.*

---

## Conversation — Natural explanation
"There is a set of context and skills that somebody could take and adapt and build their own skills on top of for their own business."
*Why it's representative: Unscripted, natural, zero posturing. How Simon talks when not writing copy — clear and practical.*

## Conversation — Warm differentiator
"It's all written in a business's brand voice, and it's a complete system, a complete companion that somebody can use day to day that knows them well."
*Why it's representative: "Complete companion" and "knows them well" show the warm, relational side. "Day to day" grounds it.*

---

## Discovery call — Opener
"Thanks for taking the time to chat today. I'd love to understand what's driving you to look at automation right now. Help me get a picture of what a typical week looks like for your team."
*Why it's representative: Warm, consultative, genuinely curious. Not pitching — diagnosing. "Help me get a picture" is collaborative.*

## Discovery call — Sharing experience
"That's really similar to what we saw with [client]. They were spending about the same amount of time on [task], and what we found was..."
*Why it's representative: Validates their problem, connects to real experience, uses "we" naturally. Not showing off — building trust.*
